mnemonics.title: Mean and Variance Recall
mnemonics.content: "One over Lambda" -- Mean = 1/λ, Variance = (1/λ)². Think: the mean is one-over-lambda, and variance is just the mean squared. Both use the same building block 1/λ.

mnemonics.title: Memoryless = No Aging
mnemonics.content: Think of a brand-new light bulb: no matter how long it has been on, the probability of it lasting another hour is always the same. The exponential distribution 'forgets' the past. This is like a coin flip -- past flips do not affect future ones. The key formula is P(X > s+t | X > s) = P(X > t).

mnemonics.title: Poisson Counts, Exponential Waits
mnemonics.content: "Poisson COUNTS fish, Exponential WAITS for the next fish." If you catch fish at rate λ per hour (Poisson), the waiting time between catches is Exp(λ). Poisson = how many; Exponential = how long.
